page: Amazon Web Services c7a.16xlarge is a compute-optimized server instance powered by dedicated AMD EPYC 9R14 processors. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it features 64 physical cores and 64 vCPUs running at a clock speed of 3.7 GHz, with a single thread per core configuration. The hardware includes 128.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory operating at 5 GHz, providing 2.0 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, relying on network-attached storage and featuring a baseline network bandwidth of 25 Gbps. It is managed via the AWS Nitro hypervisor.

Benchmark results indicate top-tier performance in multi-core CPU execution, software compilation, and ray tracing, all placing in the top 10% of tested systems. The processor's support for advanced instruction sets, including AVX-512 and vector neural network instructions, contributes to strong cryptographic and physics simulation capabilities. Memory performance is also highly competitive, with small-block read-only memory bandwidth ranking in the top 10%, while large-block read and write operations maintain strong top-25% performance.

The c7a.16xlarge demonstrates strong capabilities in database operations, static web serving, and low-latency key-value store operations, as evidenced by its top-tier Redis SET throughput. It also exhibits strong performance in large-model LLM inference tasks. Given its high compute density and lack of bundled local storage, this instance offers high qualitative cost efficiency for compute-bound workloads that do not require massive local disk space or GPU acceleration. Recommended workloads include software compilation, high-performance computing, batch processing, and medium-to-large scale web serving.
description: Amazon Web Services c7a.16xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 64 dedicated physical cores and 64 vCPUs powered by AMD EPYC 9R14 processors running at 3.7 GHz. It is equipped with 128.0 GB of DDR5 memory at 5 GHz, providing a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, and operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor. The instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, but offers a baseline network bandwidth of 25 Gbps. Benchmark data shows top-tier multi-core CPU performance, software compilation, and ray tracing, alongside strong memory bandwidth and cryptographic capabilities. The c7a.16xlarge is highly efficient for compute-intensive tasks, making it well-suited for workloads such as software compilation, batch processing, high-performance computing, and database operations.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c7a.16xlarge is a compute-optimized instance with 64 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 128 GB DDR5 RAM, and 25 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering top-tier multi-core performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c7a.16xlarge is a compute-optimized instance with 64 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 128 GB DDR5 RAM, and 25 Gbps network bandwidth.
tagline: A high-density, compute-optimized x86_64 platform featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processors and high-speed DDR5 memory for intensive multi-core workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by 64 dedicated AMD EPYC 9R14 vCPUs running at 3.7 GHz
Equipped with 128.0 GB of high-speed 5 GHz DDR5 memory
Top-tier multi-core CPU, software compilation, and ray tracing performance
Baseline network bandwidth of 25 Gbps with Nitro hypervisor integration
Best suited for compute-bound workloads, batch processing, and database operations
